 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8egw.2","title":"fcp.1password: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8egw.3","title":"TEST: fcp.1password Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8i1x4","title":"[REVIEW R2 P3][HIGH] fcp-oauth: AuthorizationSession/AuthorizationCallback Debug still leak callback-derived secrets","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:300-307 AuthorizationSession::Debug prints authorization_url verbatim even though build_auth_url at :504-560 embeds state and code_challenge into the query string; crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:804-812 AuthorizationCallback::Debug still prints attacker-controlled error_description and error_uri verbatim, while validate at :846-850 feeds the same callback fields into redacted AuthorizationError. Fix: sanitize/redact AuthorizationSession authorization_url query+fragment in Debug and redact AuthorizationCallback free-form callback strings, keeping only bounded error code.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:30:41.782607Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:30:41.782607Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8i1x4","title":"[REVIEW R2 P3][HIGH] fcp-oauth: AuthorizationSession/AuthorizationCallback Debug still leak callback-derived secrets","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:300-307 AuthorizationSession::Debug prints authorization_url verbatim even though build_auth_url at :504-560 embeds state and code_challenge into the query string; crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:804-812 AuthorizationCallback::Debug still prints attacker-controlled error_description and error_uri verbatim, while validate at :846-850 feeds the same callback fields into redacted AuthorizationError. Fix: sanitize/redact AuthorizationSession authorization_url query+fragment in Debug and redact AuthorizationCallback free-form callback strings, keeping only bounded error code.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:30:41.782607Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:41:21.321504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:41:21.320287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2850,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8i1x4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit 3013e3cf.

Two Debug-side sibling leaks that 19487b91 (AuthorizationError Display redaction) did not cover:

1. AuthorizationSession::Debug printed authorization_url verbatim; build_auth_url embeds state + PKCE code_challenge into the query. Fix: route through redacted_authorization_debug_url which Url::parse-es the URL, strips query + fragment (scheme/host/path stay legible), returns [REDACTED] on parse failure.

2. AuthorizationCallback::Debug printed error_description and error_uri verbatim. Fix: render both as [REDACTED]; short RFC 6749 §4.1.2.1 'error' enum token stays visible (matching 19487b91 policy).

Test fixes on top of the seeded diff:
- test_callback_debug used 1-letter code/state sentinels ('c', 's') that trivially collided with 'access_denied' once that was added — replaced with distinctive multi-char sentinels (SECRET_CODE_7f4a / SECRET_STATE_b19c) plus error-message-carrying asserts so future regressions report the offending Debug string.
- test_authorization_session_debug_redacts_state_and_pkce constructs an authorization_url containing the PKCE challenge, state, client_id, and a fragment so every redaction surface is exercised.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib — 621 pass vs 619 baseline (net +2 from the new redaction tests). Same 2 pre-existing broken Display-assertion tests (state_mismatch_empty_strings, test_request_token_debug) documented baseline-equivalent in 19487b91 R2 P3 session. Zero regression.

Credit: parallel agent seeded the Debug impl changes and the redacted_authorization_debug_url helper in my working tree; I committed them after fixing the coincidental single-letter assertion overlap.

— cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:41:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d1b","title":"fcp.microsoft365: Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector","description":"# FCP Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector

## Overview
Comprehensive enterprise connector for Microsoft 365 suite, providing unified access through Microsoft Graph API. Critical for enterprise adoption with support for Outlook, OneDrive, Excel, Word, OneNote, SharePoint, and MS Planner.

## Sub-Connectors Architecture

### 1. Outlook (Email & Calendar)

#### Email Operations
- **Read Messages**: Inbox, folders, search
- **Send Messages**: Compose, reply, forward
- **Manage Messages**: Move, delete, archive
- **Attachments**: Upload, download, inline
- **Mail Folders**: Create, organize, rules

#### Calendar Operations
- **Events**: Create, update, delete, RSVP
- **Calendars**: Multiple calendar support
- **Availability**: Free/busy lookup
- **Scheduling**: Find meeting times
- **Recurring Events**: Complex patterns

#### Contacts
- **Contact Management**: CRUD operations
- **Contact Folders**: Organization
- **Contact Groups**: Distribution lists

### 2. OneDrive (File Storage)

#### File Operations
- **Upload**: Simple and resumable
- **Download**: Direct and streaming
- **Copy/Move**: Within and across drives
- **Delete**: Soft delete and restore
- **Sharing**: Links, permissions

#### Folder Management
- **Create/Delete Folders**
- **List Contents**: Pagination support
- **Search**: Full-text search
- **Delta Sync**: Track changes

#### Special Folders
- Documents, Pictures, Desktop
- App-specific folders
- Shared folders

### 3. Excel Online

#### Workbook Operations
- **Open/Create Workbooks**
- **List Worksheets**
- **Workbook Sessions**: Persistent editing

#### Data Operations
```python
# Read range
GET /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10')

# Update range
PATCH /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10')
{
    "values": [["A", "B"], ["C", "D"]]
}
```

#### Table Operations
- **Create Tables**: From ranges
- **Add Rows**: Append data
- **Sort/Filter**: Data manipulation
- **Calculated Columns**: Formulas

#### Charts & Pivots
- Create charts from data
- Pivot table operations

### 4. Word Online

#### Document Operations
- **Create Documents**: From templates
- **Read Content**: Full text extraction
- **Update Content**: Append, replace
- **Export**: PDF, other formats

#### Content Controls
- Find and replace
- Bookmark management
- Comment operations

### 5. OneNote

#### Notebook Operations
- **List Notebooks**: Personal and shared
- **Create Notebooks**
- **Sections**: Create, organize
- **Section Groups**: Nested organization

#### Page Operations
```python
# Create page with HTML content
POST /sections/{id}/pages
Content-Type: text/html

<html>
  <head><title>Page Title</title></head>
  <body>
    <h1>Heading</h1>
    <p>Content here</p>
  </body>
</html>
```

#### Content
- Rich text with HTML
- Images and attachments
- Ink and handwriting
- Tags and to-do items

### 6. SharePoint

#### Site Operations
- **List Sites**: All accessible sites
- **Site Details**: Properties, permissions
- **Subsites**: Create, manage

#### Lists & Libraries
- **Document Libraries**: File storage
- **Custom Lists**: Structured data
- **List Items**: CRUD operations
- **Views**: Custom views

#### Pages & News
- **Site Pages**: Create, update
- **News Posts**: Publishing
- **Web Parts**: Content blocks

### 7. MS Planner

#### Plan Operations
- **Create Plans**: Within Groups
- **List Plans**: User's plans
- **Plan Details**: Buckets, tasks

#### Task Operations
```python
{
    "planId": "...",
    "bucketId": "...",
    "title": "Task title",
    "assignments": {
        "user-id": {
            "@odata.type": "#microsoft.graph.plannerAssignment",
            "orderHint": " !"
        }
    },
    "dueDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z"
}
```

#### Bucket Management
- Create/organize buckets
- Move tasks between buckets
- Bucket ordering

## Microsoft Graph API (Unified Endpoint)

### Base Configuration
```yaml
graph_api:
  base_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0"
  beta_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta"
  scopes:
    - "User.Read"
    - "Mail.ReadWrite"
    - "Files.ReadWrite.All"
    - "Calendars.ReadWrite"
    - "Sites.ReadWrite.All"
    - "Tasks.ReadWrite"
```

### Batch Requests
```python
POST /$batch
{
    "requests": [
        {"id": "1", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/messages"},
        {"id": "2", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/events"},
        {"id": "3", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/drive/root/children"}
    ]
}
```

## OAuth 2.0 with Refresh Tokens

### Authorization Flow
```
1. GET /authorize?client_id=...&scope=...&redirect_uri=...
2. User consents
3. POST /token (authorization_code grant)
4. Store refresh_token securely
5. POST /token (refresh_token grant) when access_token expires
```

### Token Management
```yaml
auth:
  type: "oauth2"
  client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}"
  client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}"
  tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}"
  redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback"
  
  token_storage:
    type: "encrypted_file"
    path: "/secure/tokens.enc"
    
  auto_refresh: true
  refresh_buffer_seconds: 300
```

### Scopes by Sub-connector
| Sub-connector | Required Scopes |
|---------------|-----------------|
| Outlook Mail | Mail.Read, Mail.ReadWrite, Mail.Send |
| Outlook Calendar | Calendars.Read, Calendars.ReadWrite |
| OneDrive | Files.Read, Files.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All |
| Excel | Files.ReadWrite |
| Word | Files.ReadWrite |
| OneNote | Notes.Read, Notes.ReadWrite |
| SharePoint | Sites.Read.All, Sites.ReadWrite.All |
| Planner | Tasks.Read, Tasks.ReadWrite, Group.ReadWrite.All |

## Webhook Subscriptions (Real-time Events)

### Subscription Management
```python
# Create subscription
POST /subscriptions
{
    "changeType": "created,updated,deleted",
    "notificationUrl": "https://webhook.example.com/m365",
    "resource": "/me/messages",
    "expirationDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z",
    "clientState": "secret-validation-token"
}
```

### Supported Resources
- `/me/messages`: Email changes
- `/me/events`: Calendar changes
- `/me/contacts`: Contact changes
- `/drives/{id}/root`: OneDrive changes
- `/sites/{id}/lists/{id}/items`: SharePoint changes
- `/groups/{id}/planner/tasks`: Planner changes

### Webhook Handler
```python
async def handle_webhook(request):
    # Validation challenge
    if 'validationToken' in request.query:
        return PlainTextResponse(request.query['validationToken'])
    
    # Process notifications
    for notification in request.json()['value']:
        resource = notification['resource']
        change_type = notification['changeType']
        # Process change...
```

### Subscription Renewal
- Max lifetime: 3 days (most resources)
- Auto-renewal before expiration
- Webhook validation handling

## Configuration Schema
```yaml
fcp.microsoft365:
  tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}"
  client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}"
  client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}"
  
  auth:
    type: "oauth2"
    redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback"
    token_cache: "/secure/token_cache.json"
    
  sub_connectors:
    outlook:
      enabled: true
      mail_folders: ["Inbox", "Sent Items"]
      calendar_sync: true
      
    onedrive:
      enabled: true
      root_path: "/Documents"
      
    excel:
      enabled: true
      
    word:
      enabled: true
      
    onenote:
      enabled: true
      
    sharepoint:
      enabled: true
      sites: ["https://tenant.sharepoint.com/sites/team"]
      
    planner:
      enabled: true
      
  webhooks:
    enabled: true
    endpoint_url: "https://webhook.example.com/m365"
    resources:
      - "/me/messages"
      - "/me/events"
      
  rate_limits:
    requests_per_second: 10
    concurrent_connections: 5
```

## MCP Tool Definitions

### Outlook Tools
- `outlook_list_messages`: List emails
- `outlook_send_message`: Send email
- `outlook_create_event`: Create calendar event
- `outlook_get_availability`: Check free/busy

### OneDrive Tools
- `onedrive_list_files`: List directory contents
- `onedrive_upload_file`: Upload file
- `onedrive_download_file`: Download file
- `onedrive_share_file`: Create sharing link

### Excel Tools
- `excel_read_range`: Read cell range
- `excel_write_range`: Write to cells
- `excel_create_table`: Create data table
- `excel_run_function`: Execute formula

### SharePoint Tools
- `sharepoint_list_sites`: List sites
- `sharepoint_get_list_items`: Read list data
- `sharepoint_create_list_item`: Add list item

### Planner Tools
- `planner_list_tasks`: List user tasks
- `planner_create_task`: Create new task
- `planner_update_task`: Update task status

### Webhook Tools
- `m365_create_subscription`: Create webhook
- `m365_list_subscriptions`: List active webhooks
- `m365_delete_subscription`: Remove webhook

## Enterprise Features

### Multi-tenant Support
- Per-tenant configuration
- Tenant isolation
- Cross-tenant operations (with consent)

### Compliance & Governance
- Audit logging
- Data residency awareness
- eDiscovery integration

### Delegated vs Application Permissions
- User context operations
- Background service operations
- Admin consent flows

## Error Handling
- Token refresh failures
- Rate limit (429) handling
- Throttling with Retry-After
- Permission denied errors
- Resource not found

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Core Auth (Week 1-2)
- [ ] OAuth 2.0 flow implementation
- [ ] Token storage and refresh
- [ ] Multi-tenant support
- [ ] Permission management

### Phase 2: Outlook (Week 3-4)
- [ ] Mail operations
- [ ] Calendar operations
- [ ] Contact operations

### Phase 3: OneDrive & Files (Week 5-6)
- [ ] File operations
- [ ] Excel integration
- [ ] Word integration

### Phase 4: Collaboration (Week 7-8)
- [ ] SharePoint integration
- [ ] OneNote integration
- [ ] Planner integration

### Phase 5: Real-time (Week 9-10)
- [ ] Webhook subscriptions
- [ ] Change notifications
- [ ] Delta sync

### Phase 6: Enterprise (Week 11-12)
- [ ] Compliance features
- [ ] Monitoring
- [ ] Documentation

## Dependencies
- msal (Microsoft Authentication Library)
- httpx (async HTTP client)
- cryptography (token encryption)
- pydantic (data validation)

## Success Metrics
- OAuth flow success rate > 99%
- API call latency < 500ms
- Webhook delivery success > 99.9%
- Support all listed sub-connectors

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","title":"fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research","description":"# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way.

This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely.

## Scope
### Research macros (compositions)
- `reddit.research.search_threads`:
  - input: query + subreddit scope + time window
  - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps)
- `reddit.research.collect_evidence`:
  - input: thread ids
  - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids)

### Safety/bounds
- Explicit caps:
  - max threads
  - max comments per thread
  - max total bytes returned
- NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`
- `reddit.research` (macro operations)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- All content is tainted.
- Structured logs include only ids + counts.

## Deliverables
- Canonical output types:
  - `ResearchThreadSummary`
  - `ResearchEvidenceBundle`
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.research.search_threads`
  - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - ranking determinism
  - bounds enforcement
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Research macros are deterministic, bounded, and mechanically authorized.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","title":"fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution","description":"# fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe visibility into Terraform module structure so agents can:
- inspect required providers/modules
- analyze dependency graph
- prepare plans with correct initialization

## Scope
- Run `terraform init` in a controlled, non-interactive mode (may download modules/providers).
- List required providers and versions.
- Enumerate modules and sources.
- Produce a canonical `ModuleGraph` summary.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.modules.read`
- `terraform.init` (dangerous-ish if it downloads content; policy-gated)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace
  - restrict process execution
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless module registries are explicitly allowed.
- No automatic downloads unless policy explicitly permits.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.providers.list`
  - `terraform.modules.graph`
  - Optional: `terraform.init`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ModuleGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with fixtures for:
  - module parsing
  - provider list parsing
  - policy denial of init without explicit capability

## Acceptance criteria
- Module graph summaries are deterministic and safe-by-default.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-djx","title":"fcp.airtable: Base and table discovery API","description":"# fcp.airtable: Base and Table Discovery API

## Goal
Implement Airtable discovery operations so agents can understand what data exists before attempting record CRUD.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.bases.list`
- `airtable.bases.get` (schema)
- `airtable.tables.list`
- `airtable.tables.get`
- `airtable.fields.list`

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`airtable.schema.read`).
- Prefer stable identifiers:
  - support referencing fields by **field id** (stable across renames) and name (human-readable).
- Cache schema responses with bounded TTL to reduce API load.
- Strict input validation:
  - base/table identifiers canonicalized
  - reject ambiguous references

## Tests
- Mock-only API responses for:
  - schema changes (rename fields, change options)
  - permission failures
  - rate limits
- Logs are structured JSON (redacted).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema discovery is deterministic and stable across renames.
- Conformance/E2E runner can validate default deny + allow paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mostw","title":"[REVIEW-CONVERGE][HIGH] fcp-host dispatcher can misroute late subprocess replies after timeout","description":"P2 converge finding from commit 58e25d0e. Evidence: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:194-199 creates a JSON-RPC request id, but the response path at 234-239 ignores ids entirely and accepts any next line's `result`. ConnectorProcessRunner::read_json at 814-829 applies a 10s stdout timeout and request() at 832-835 returns that timeout without draining or resynchronizing stdout. Bug scenario: request A times out in read_json, the connector emits A's response later, then request B is sent and read_json consumes A's stale response as B because no id match is checked. That can misattribute invoke/handshake/health replies across logical calls and poison the connector session after one slow RPC. Suggested fix: carry the expected request id through ConnectorRpcRequest/request(), validate the response id before accepting it, and treat any timed-out or mismatched response as a poisoned transport that must be restarted or hard-drained before issuing another RPC; simple timeout errors are not enough because JSON-RPC framing has already desynchronized.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T22:09:05.507240Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:48:01.748890Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:48:01.748602Z","close_reason":"Completed: fcp-host subprocess RPC now generates and validates per-request JSON-RPC ids, rejects mismatched responses, skips stale lower-epoch replies after timeouts, and adds regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-n6r","title":"fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval)","description":"# fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval) (FCP2)

## Goal
Make Terraform operations mechanically safe:
- plan is always read-only
- apply is always approval-gated and bound to a specific plan hash
- destroy is forbidden by default

This bead defines the safety envelope that all other terraform operations must obey.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Core safety rules (non-negotiable)
- Never run Terraform with `-auto-approve`.
- Always run with `-input=false`.
- Apply requires:
  - a previously generated plan reference
  - a matching plan hash
  - an ApprovalToken when policy requires
- Explicit denial reason codes for:
  - missing plan
  - hash mismatch
  - drift detected
  - destroy forbidden

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.plan`
- `terraform.apply` (dangerous; approval required)
- `terraform.destroy` (critical; disabled unless explicitly enabled)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace allowlist
  - restrict process execution to terraform binary
- Audit:
  - all state reads are audited
  - all applies produce receipts + audit events

## Deliverables
- Safety gate layer used by all terraform operations.
- Canonical DecisionReceipt reason codes for denials.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests for:
  - refusal of auto-approve
  - refusal of apply without plan
  - hash mismatch denial
  - destroy forbidden default
  - audit event emission on state reads

## Acceptance criteria
- It is mechanically impossible to apply/destroy without explicit authorization.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-n8tr","title":"[IDEA-22.1] Implement posture attestation verification + policy hooks","description":"## What
Add posture attestation schema + verification, and integrate with policy evaluation.

## How
- Define schema + validation rules in `fcp-core`/`fcp-conformance`.
- Extend policy engine to enforce `requires_posture` in ZonePolicy.
- Emit DecisionReceipts with evidence on denial.

## Success Criteria
